metaTitle: Boiler Leaking Water | Common Causes and Next Steps
metaDescription: Boiler leaking water? Learn what it may mean, what you can safely check and when to book an urgent repair.
h1: Why Is My Boiler Leaking Water?
intro: If water is leaking from your boiler or from pipework directly around it, the leak should be taken seriously. The source may be small at first, but it usually points to a fault that needs repair.
safetyWarning: Leaks around a boiler should be treated as urgent, especially if the pressure is dropping or electrics are nearby.
symptoms: Visible dripping from the boiler or discharge pipe
safeChecks: Check where the leak appears to be coming from without opening the casing
Look at the pressure gauge if it is safe to access
Move valuables away from any active dripping
doNotDiy: Do not open the casing or tighten internal components
Do not ignore water around electrical parts or controls
Do not keep using the system if the leak is active and worsening
typicalFixes: Repairing a failed seal or valve
Resolving internal component leaks after diagnosis

sections.heading: What a boiler leak can mean
sections.body: Leaks can come from pressure-related parts, worn seals, damaged components or pipework connections near the appliance. The visible drip is often only the symptom.

sections.heading: Why leaking boilers should be prioritised
sections.body: A leak can lead to pressure loss, repeated shutdowns and damage around the appliance, and it may get worse quickly once the boiler heats up.

sections.heading: When to book an urgent repair
sections.body: If the boiler is actively leaking, pressure is falling or the leak is near electrics, book an urgent repair visit rather than waiting to see if it settles down.
